statement.en: Upgrading in global value chains from suppliers’ perspective: cases of Bangladeshi tier-1 suppliers
statement.summary: This thesis takes a granular approach to investigate the objectives and strategies of tier-1 suppliers for achieving economic and non-economic (social and environmental) upgrading. The extant global value chain (GVC) literature predominantly takes a ‘top-down’ view to study economic upgrading as an outcome based on how GVCs are governed by lead firms, albeit with the recent increasing interest in taking a bottom-up perspective. However, the bottom-up upgrading studies do not take the objectives of suppliers into account and only focus on economic upgrading without paying much attention on social and environmental upgrading, when studying upgrading from the suppliers’ perspective. Using multiple case studies of eight Bangladeshi apparel tier-1 suppliers – categorised into two broad groups:

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a doctoral thesis (dated 2026, future-dated or typo) presenting empirical case study research. The claims are provisional pending peer review, publication, and validation against GVC literature. The research question (supplier upgrading strategies) is substantively addressable but the specific findings are not yet externally validated.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: サプライヤーの昇進は経済・社会・環境の三つの力の張力から生まれ、その調和のコストが全てを決める。
